props.title: Fourteen Workloads, One Question — A Day in the Life of a Pre-Trade Scenario Sweep

props.text: Google added webhooks to Gemini in May to kill polling for long-running AI jobs. But when your "fleet" is fourteen long-running sims on someone else's server, you still have to poll — the question is how.
PR #150 (opened today on MiroShark) introduces POST /api/simulation/batch-status: one call, list semantics, byte-identical envelopes for private vs unknown IDs (a dedicated test enforces it).
A five-person desk's pre-trade scenario sweep drops from 420 hourly polls to 30 without exposing whether someone else's private sim exists.
